questionHtml:
The energy from burning 0.250 g of ethanol causes the temperature of 150 cm3 of water to rise by 10.5 °C. What is the enthalpy of combustion of ethanol, in kJ mol–1?

Specific heat capacity of water: 4.18 J g–1 K–1.

A.  150×4.18×10.50.25046.08

B.  150×4.18×10.50.25046.08×1000

C.  150×4.18×273+10.50.25046.08

D.  150×4.18×273+10.50.25046.08×1000

meta.title: The energy from burning 0.250 g of ethanol causes the temperature of 150 cm3 of water to rise by 10.5 °C. What is the enthalpy of combustion of ethanol, in kJ mol–1? Specific heat
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 1.1.4—The standard enthalpy change for a chemical reaction, ΔH⦵, refers to the heat transferred at constant pressure under standard conditions and states. It can be determined from the change in temperature of a pure substance. Apply the equations Q = mcΔT and ΔH = − Qn in the calculation of the enthalpy change of a reaction.
